message.content:
Review the quality of a markdown content rewrite for a personal CV site.

WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: Rewrote src/content/list/cv.md — the sole content source for a personal CV website. Changed from old format with "Responsibilities:" prose to achievement-bullet format with quantified metrics. Added Core Competencies section. Fixed typo in title. Reorganized Technical Skills.

PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task 1 from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-13-cv-upgrade.md in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/cv-astro

BASE_SHA: fa2d992
HEAD_SHA: 70d158f

DESCRIPTION: Content-only change to a markdown file. No logic, no tests applicable. Review for: markdown correctness, content quality (does it read well for an EM/CTO/Architect audience), any obvious formatting issues that would break the Astro markdown renderer.

Work from: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/cv-astro

content.text: I'll start by reading the plan document and the current state of the CV file to do a thorough review.

content.content:
1	### Andrii Popenko. Engineering Manager. Software Architect.
2	
3	---
4	
5	### Contact information
6	
7	- **Location**: Barcelona, Spain
8	- **Email**: [aopopenko@gmail.com](mailto:aopopenko@gmail.com)
9	- **GitHub**: [github.com/4thwithme](https://github.com/4thwithme)
10	- **LinkedIn**: [linkedin.com/in/andrii-popenko-3331b3193/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrii-popenko-3331b3193/)
11	
12	---
13	
14	### Summary
15	
16	Engineering Manager and Software Architect with 9+ years of experience building high-scale e-commerce systems. Led a team of 8 engineers on a platform processing 12,000+ orders/day. Architected a recommendations engine serving 40M+ queries/month at 99.99% availability. Combines deep technical expertise (TypeScript, React, Node.js, Python, ML) with proven leadership: hiring 20+ engineers, driving technical strategy, and delivering measurable business outcomes.
17	
18	---
19	
20	### Core Competencies
21	
22	Engineering Leadership · Software Architecture · Technical Strategy & Roadmapping · High-Load Systems Design · E-commerce Platforms · ML / Recommendation Systems · Team Scaling & Hiring · Cross-functional Collaboration · Performance Engineering · Agile / Scrum · Code Review & Mentoring · Stakeholder Management
23	
24	---
25	
26	### Experience
27	
28	- #### [01.2024 - present]: Engineering Manager at "**Aleph1**"
29	
30	  **Location**: Barcelona, Spain
31	
32	  - Led a team of 8 engineers delivering features on a high-volume e-commerce platform processing **12,000+ orders/day**
33	  - Architected a high-load recommendations service handling **40M+ queries/month** at **99.99% availability** with zero unplanned downtime
34	  - Designed and deployed 4 recommendation models: FBT, similar items, Fourier-based trend prediction, and occasion-based vector search — driving a **40% increase in recommendation-attributed purchases**
35	  - Built trending products model from scratch using Fourier transforms for time-series trend prediction across the full product catalog
36	  - Scaled engineering team: hired **20+ engineers**, conducted **200+ technical interviews**
37	  - Drove RFC process, architectural decisions, and cross-team technical strategy
38	  - Owned incident response for production issues within team's domain
39	
40	- #### [06.2020 - 01.2024]: Senior Software Engineer at "**Aleph1**"
41	
42	  **Location**: Barcelona, Spain
43	
44	  - Contributed to e-commerce platform growth from early stage to current scale
45	  - Resolved complex reliability, resilience, and performance challenges
46	  - Designed and built the React design system and component library
47	  - Conducted code reviews and mentored junior engineers
48	
49	- #### [05.2019 - 06.2020]: Software Engineer at "**Shwartzcode**"
50	
51	  **Location**: Kyiv, Ukraine
52

54	  - Implemented design system and component library for a call-centre application

64	  **Location**: Kyiv, Ukraine — Built client websites with WordPress and PHP

67

69

71	
72	  **Location**: Kyiv, Ukraine — Taught robotics and programming; led school curriculum as senior teacher
73	
74	- #### [09.2019 - 11.2021]: Teacher of Programming at "**Main Academy**"
75	
76	  **Location**: Kyiv, Ukraine — Taught HTML, CSS, JS, React; prepared students for their first engineering roles
77

83	- **ML / Data**: Recommendation systems, Fourier analysis, vector search, ElasticSearch

Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking. 4 5 **Goal:** Rewrite CV content with ATS keywords and quantified achievements, create a clean professional PDF template, and wire it to the existing download endpoint. 6 7 **Architecture:** `src/utils/cv-html.ts` exports a single `getCvHtml()` function that returns a complete self-contained HTML document. `src/pages/cv-pdf.astro` renders it as a browser-previewable page. `src/pages/andrii-popenko-cv.js` passes the same HTML to `html-pdf-node`. `src/content/list/cv.md` is updated separately for the dark-themed website. 8 9 **Tech Stack:** Astro 4, TypeScript, html-pdf-node, Tailwind (website only — PDF template uses inline CSS) 10 11 --- 12 13 ## File Map 14 15 | File | Action | Responsibility | 16 |------|--------|----------------| 17 | `src/content/list/cv.md` | Modify | Website CV content (dark theme, markdown) | 18 | `src/utils/cv-html.ts` | Create | Self-contained HTML+CSS string for ATS-safe PDF | 19 | `src/pages/cv-pdf.astro` | Create | Browser preview of the clean PDF template | 20 | `src/pages/andrii-popenko-cv.js` | Modify | Replace temp/index.html read with getCvHtml() | 21 22 `src/components/Header.astro` — no changes needed, download link already present. 23 24 --- 25 26 ## Task 1: Rewrite cv.md for the website 27 28 **Files:** 29 - Modify: `src/content/list/cv.md` 30 31 - [ ] **Step 1: Replace the entire file content** 32 33 ```markdown 34 ### Andrii Popenko. content.text: Now let me check the git diff to see exactly what changed and look at how the Astro site consumes this markdown file.

input.description: Show exact diff of cv.md between base and head SHA

content.content:
diff --git a/src/content/list/cv.md b/src/content/list/cv.md index fe5f97d..501b96a 100644 --- a/src/content/list/cv.md +++ b/src/content/list/cv.md @@ -1,4 +1,4 @@ -### Andrii Popenko. Engeneering Manager. Team Lead. +### Andrii Popenko. Engineering Manager. Software Architect. --- @@ -13,88 +13,90 @@ ### Summary -I have 8+ years of development experience. My main traits are: problem solving (coding), curiosity, perseverance and empathy. As an engineering manager, in my work I focus on: the product quality and code (because I have high standard expectations from UX/UI), well-organized teamwork, healthy and friendly atmosphere in the team, and the result (result delivered on time). I'm an easily challenged person, the more difficult the task in front of me is, the more enthusiastically I tackle it. I like learning new technologies. Have deep expertise in front end development, back end, databases, UX/UI. +Engineering Manager and Software Architect with 9+ years of experience building high-scale e-commerce systems. Led a team of 8 engineers on a platform processing 12,000+ orders/day. Architected a recommendations engine serving 40M+ queries/month at 99.99% availability. Combines deep technical expertise (TypeScript, React, Node.js, Python, ML) with proven leadership: hiring 20+ engineers, driving technical strategy, and delivering measurable business outcomes. --- -### Technologies +### Core Competencies -- **Front end**: HTML, CSS (SCSS), JS (Typescript), Three.js, Next.js, Astro.js, GrapthQL, React (+ other), canvas drawing, webgl, pixel perfect, React design systems, accessibility, creation component libraries, Wordpress. -- **Back end**: Node.js, Nest.js, Express, Bun.js, RabbitMQ, socket, Meteor, GrapthQL (Apollo). -- **Infrastructure**: CircleCI, Webpack, Kubernetes, Gulp, AWS, Docker, GH Actions, Chai, Mocha, Sinon. -- **Databases**: MongoDB, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Redis, ElasticSearch, Neo4j. -- **Other languages**: Fortran, Python, C++, PHP, Rust. -- **Other**: GIT, Kibana, Logstash, Postman, npm. +Engineering Leadership · Software Architecture · Technical Strategy & Roadmapping · High-Load Systems Design · E-commerce Platforms · ML / Recommendation Systems · Team Scaling & Hiring · Cross-functional Collaboration · Performance Engineering · Agile / Scrum · Code Review & Mentoring · Stakeholder Management --- ### Experience -- #### [01.2024 - present]: Engineering Manager at "**Aleph1**". +- #### [01.2024 - present]: Engineering Manager at "**Aleph1**" - **Location**: Barcelona, Spain

-[09.2016 - 06.2018]: Master of nuclear power engineering. +- **Primary**: TypeScript, JavaScript, React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, Python +- **ML / Data**: Recommendation systems, Fourier analysis, vector search, ElasticSearch +- **Architecture**: Microservices, high-load system design, REST, GraphQL, RabbitMQ, WebSockets +- **Infrastructure**: AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, CircleCI +- **Databases**: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, ElasticSearch, Neo4j, MySQL +- **Other**: Git, Kibana, Logstash, Webpack, Astro.js --- -### Languages +### Education + +**National Technical University of Ukraine "Igor Sikorsky Kyiv Polytechnic Institute"** -English, Ukrainian. +- [09.2016 - 06.2018]: Master of Nuclear Power Engineering +- [09.2012 - 06.2016]: Bachelor of Nuclear Power Engineering --- -### Courses - -- Prog.it: HTML, CSS, JavaScript -- javascript.info - Node.js, React -- Stepik.org - Python -- MongoDB University - M001 -- MongoDB University - M121 -- MongoDB University - M201 -- Udemy - Logstash, Kibana -- Udemy - ElasticSearch -- GraphAcademy - Neo4j, Cypher -- GraphAcademy - Graph Data Modeling -- Robot_dreams - Microservice architecture +### Languages + +English (professional), Ukrainian (native)

content.text:
I have everything I need. Here is the full review. --- ## Code Review: `src/content/list/cv.md` — Task 1 ### Overall Assessment The implementation matches the plan specification exactly. The file content in the repo is a character-for-character match with the markdown block in Task 1, Step 1 of the plan. The task is complete and correct. What follows is a detailed breakdown of quality across the dimensions you asked for. --- ### Plan Alignment PASS. Every item the plan specified was delivered: - Title typo fixed: "Engeneering" corrected to "Engineering Manager. Software Architect." (was "Team Lead.") - Summary replaced with the achievement-and-metric paragraph from the plan - Core Competencies section added with the exact 12 dot-separated competencies - Experience rewritten in achievement-bullet format with bolded metrics - Technical Skills reorganised from the old flat "Technologies" section into the six categorised rows - Education promoted above Languages, degree entries capitalised consistently - Languages updated from bare "English, Ukrainian." to proficiency qualifiers - Courses section removed (was not in the plan — correct omission) --- ### Markdown Correctness and Astro Renderer Safety PASS with one minor structural note. The site renders the markdown via `post.render()` inside a Tailwind `prose` container in `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/cv-astro/src/layouts/PostDetails.astro`. Everything in the new file is standard CommonMark: - All `
` and `` raw HTML tags from the old file are gone. This is an improvement — the old file relied on raw HTML for line breaks inside list items, which is fragile in strict parsers. - Heading hierarchy: all section headings are `###` (h3). Sub-entries use `####` (h4) inside list items. This is an unusual but technically valid structure that Astro/remark processes without errors. The `prose` class will style nested headings. No breaking change. - The `&` character appears literally in two places: `Technical Strategy & Roadmapping` in Core Competencies (line 22) and `Robotics & Programming` in the experience entry (line 70). In CommonMark body text this is fine. In HTML attributes it would require escaping, but these are not in attributes. No issue. - The `---` horizontal rules used as section separators are valid thematic breaks throughout. One structural observation that is not a blocker: the experience entries for the four older/non-software roles (Mamontova Studio, SSTC NRS, Robocode, Main Academy) use an inline pattern — `**Location**: Kyiv, Ukraine — [description text]` — while the three primary roles use a proper sub-list. This mixed pattern comes directly from the plan spec and is intentional for density. The prose container will render both forms correctly. It is worth noting for future edits. --- ### Content Quality for an EM / CTO / Architect Audience STRONG. The rewrite is a meaningful upgrade over the old content. What works well: - The summary leads with concrete scale (12K orders/day, 40M queries/month, 99.99% availability) in the first two sentences. This is exactly what a hiring manager scans for. - "200+ technical interviews" for 20+ hires is an unusual but credible specificity that signals genuine EM experience, not just a title change. - The four named ML models (FBT, similar items, Fourier-based trend prediction, occasion-based vector search) plus the 40% purchase attribution figure is the strongest single bullet on the page — it demonstrates both architecture depth and product awareness simultaneously. - Technical Skills is now categorised by role dimension (Primary / ML-Data / Architecture / Infrastructure / Databases) rather than the old Front end / Back end split, which maps better to how an architect or CTO reads a skill list. - Core Competencies reads naturally as a keyword band for both ATS parsing and human skimming. Items that could be improved (suggestions, not blockers): **Suggestion 1 — Senior SSE period lacks a standout metric.** The Aleph1 Senior SSE entry (June 2020 – January 2024, 3.5 years) has four bullets, none with a quantified outcome. For an audience that just read "40M queries/month" in the next role, this section reads as noticeably weaker. Even one retrospective metric (e.g., page load improvement, number of components shipped, adoption breadth of the design system) would balance the section. This is especially important because this role represents the majority of the subject's software engineering career. **Suggestion 2 — "9+ years" in the summary will need manual updating.** The summary opens with "9+ years of experience." Given the start date is June 2016, this is currently accurate (April 2026). However, it is a hardcoded string. If the CV site remains live for another year, it will be stale. Worth noting so it is tracked as a maintenance item. **Suggestion 3 — "call-centre" spelling.** Line 54 uses `call-centre` (British English). The rest of the document uses American English conventions (e.g., "practise" is not present, but "organization," "mentoring," etc. are American). This is minor but worth aligning if the primary audience is US or international-tech. **Suggestion 4 — The Hasky entry has only one bullet.** `- Developed and maintained web services using React and Node.js` is a single weak bullet with no differentiation. As an early-career role this is acceptable, but if anything can be added (e.g., scale, domain, team size), it would strengthen the timeline. --- ### Formatting Issues That Could Break the Renderer None found. Specifically: - No unclosed Markdown constructs - No mixed indentation in the nested list structure - No orphaned bold or italic markers - The frontmatter-less format is consistent with how the `list` collection is consumed — `getCollection("list")` fetches it without requiring frontmatter, and `post.render()` renders the body directly - The file ends at line 102 with `English (professional), Ukrainian (native)` followed by a trailing newline — correct --- ### Summary of Issues by Severity Critical: None. Important: - None that block this task, but the stale "9+ years" string (Suggestion 2) should be tracked — it will become incorrect within roughly 12 months. Suggestions: - Add at least one quantified metric to the Senior SSE Aleph1 bullets (Suggestion 1) — highest priority of the suggestions - Align "call-centre" to American spelling if targeting US/international audiences (Suggestion 3) - Strengthen or expand the single-bullet Hasky entry if any detail is available (Suggestion 4) --- The implementation is clean, correctly matches the plan, and is safe to ship as-is. The suggestions above are improvements for a future pass, not rework requirements for this task.
